column1: # AccuracySNES rendered-scene goldens (docs/adr/0013).
# Format: <scene id>\t<hash>
# The hash is FNV-1a over a fixed 256x224 region of canonical 0RRRRRGGGGGBBBBB pixels. Fixed and
# canonical because emulators agree about neither geometry nor pixel format, and a golden has to
# compare pictures rather than output conventions. Each host declares where its own picture starts
# in its own buffer (`FIRST_ROW`): RustySNES 0, snes9x's libretro core 0, Mesen2 7.
# ADR 0013 rule 4: a golden may be blessed ONLY from a cross-validated render — a scene the
# reference emulators have been shown to agree on, with the agreement recorded here. A scene with
# no entry is reported as unblessed and does not fail the gate, because an unblessed scene is not
# yet evidence of anything.
# Separate from undisbeliever-framebuffer.tsv on purpose: these are first-party and may be
# regenerated when WE intend a behaviour change; those are third-party and may not. Mixing them
# invites applying the wrong rule to the wrong file.
# Cross-validated three ways on the identical image, all agreeing bit-for-bit:
#   * RustySNES        crates/rustysnes-test-harness/tests/accuracysnes_scenes.rs
#   * snes9x (libretro) scripts/accuracysnes/libretro_crossval.c --scenes
#   * Mesen2 (headless) scripts/accuracysnes/mesen_scenes.lua
# These are the first three scenes the oracle ever ran, and all three initially disagreed with the
# references. Both causes were RustySNES bugs, not scene bugs, and both are fixed in the same
# change (see CHANGELOG and docs/ppu.md):
#   1. the BG vertical fetch was a line late — the first displayed line must show BG row
#      BGnVOFS+1, not row 0;
#   2. mosaic quantised the BG's own row rather than the screen row, so a mosaic block moved with
#      the scroll instead of staying anchored to the picture.
# Independent corroboration: agreement with snes9x across the 29-ROM undisbeliever suite went from
# 2/29 to 14/29 with those two fixes in place.
c5-mode1-bg-priority
c8-fixed-colour-add
c10-mosaic-4x
